prose:
Frontier-orbital (HOMO–LUMO) gap of formaldehyde — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.  
Geometry: fixed experimental/formatted structure supplied by user (`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz`).  This run was a single-point energy/orbital evaluation at that geometry (notation: DFT//given xyz).  
SCF converged in 8 cycles; no engine warnings were reported (`warnings` array empty).  Integrity checks report status `ok`; trustworthy = true.  
Live calculation log was written during execution to a `frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out` file in the current working directory; the exact command executed (as echoed in the result JSON’s `cli_invocation`) was:
```
/…/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out …/live.json
```
Headline result (Koopmans-style orbital gap): 5.8696712 eV.  No implicit solvent model was requested; calculation performed in the gas phase.  
No warnings reported.
